equivalence_argument: Because the registry service spawn is absent, there is no spawned-task error handling that logs failures while allowing BuilderContext construction.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: There is no registry service startup failure path that logs errors without aborting BuilderContext construction.
related_but_insufficient_code: BuilderContext::new is a const constructor with no async service spawn.
No audited repo defines ExecutorRegistryService::start, so no runtime failure can be caught and logged by builder startup.

rationale: The service is started inside executor.spawn and the only visible error handling is a tracing::error! inside the async task, so startup of the builder context is decoupled from registry service runtime failure. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: If the background ExecutorRegistryService::start call returns an error, BuilderContext::new logs the error from the spawned task rather than failing or preventing the BuilderContext from being constructed.
suggested_falsification_tests: Force ExecutorRegistryService::start to return an error in a BuilderContext::new test and assert BuilderContext::new still returns while an error log is emitted from the spawned task.
summary: Registry service failure handling is absent with the service itself.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Force ExecutorRegistryService::start to return an error and assert BuilderContext::new still returns while logging; current code lacks the service and error-handling path.
title: Registry service failures do not abort builder construction
